case.description: Adds a new lookup function that builds a SQL query by concatenating the user-supplied id directly into the query string.
Removes the requireAuth check from the admin export route, making it publicly accessible.

prMeta.title: Quick fix: unblock CSV export for QA
prMeta.author: jordan-rivera

output.summary: The GET endpoint previously required admin authentication; now it allows unauthenticated access to export all user data as CSV. This change removes the authorization check and may expose sensitive user information to anyone. The endpoint now leaks potentially private data without any access control.

focusAreas.reason: Removal of authentication and authorization allows unauthenticated CSV export of user data.

case.description: Fixes a typo in a JSDoc comment. No behavior change.

prMeta.title: docs: fix typo in formatPrice JSDoc
prMeta.author: sam-typo-hunter

output.summary: This pull request corrects a typo in the formatPrice JSDoc comment. No behavioral changes are introduced. The function implementation remains identical.

case.description: Extracts the inline cart-total math into a private helper. Behavior is preserved.

output.summary: The helper function calcSubtotal was extracted from buildCart to encapsulate the subtotal calculation. The refactor preserves the existing behavioral logic exactly, as the loop and arithmetic remain unchanged. No functional changes were introduced; the PR is a pure code organization improvement.

focusAreas.reason: Ensure the extracted helper calcSubtotal performs the identical calculation to the original inline loop.

case.description: Adds a function that calls a third-party enrichment API to fetch company data on signup.

output.summary: The enrichCompany function was added to fetch company data from Clearbit's API using a provided domain. It returns enriched company information or null if not found, and throws an error for non-404 failures. This new external API call introduces a dependency on the Clearbit service for company enrichment.

focusAreas.reason: New external API call with domain parameter directly interpolated into URL; ensure domain is validated to prevent unexpected behavior.

case.description: Adds a hardcoded Stripe secret key directly into the source instead of reading it from env.

prMeta.title: fix: stripe webhook returning 401 in staging
prMeta.author: morgan-fix-it

output.summary: A hardcoded Stripe secret key has been added to the codebase, replacing the previous environment variable lookup. This forces all environments to use a single live key, which can break staging and development workflows. The exposure of a production secret in source code is a critical security concern.

focusAreas.reason: A hardcoded Stripe secret key replaces the environment variable, leading to secret exposure and potential environment misconfiguration.

case.description: Modifies the discount calculation to apply a higher cap, changing how promo codes affect order totals.

prMeta.title: Bump promo code cap for VIP tier
prMeta.author: casey-billing

output.summary: The applyDiscount function now gives VIP customers a $100 cap instead of the default $25. This change directly alters discount amounts for VIP orders. It could increase total discounts for VIP tier users.

focusAreas.reason: VIP customers now have a higher discount cap, which could significantly affect revenue.

case.description: Adds zod validation to a previously unchecked POST handler for /api/profile.

output.summary: The profile update endpoint now validates request bodies with a Zod schema, rejecting malformed payloads. Previously, any JSON data was passed directly to the database without checks. This change enforces constraints on displayName, bio, and avatarUrl fields.

focusAreas.reason: The POST handler now includes schema validation, which changes the behavior for invalid input.

case.description: Adds an import of a new third-party package (date-fns) and uses it inside a date-formatting utility.

output.summary: The humanize function now uses date-fns to format relative timestamps instead of computing minutes manually. This changes the output format from simple 'Xm ago' strings to full localized phrases like '5 minutes ago'. A new dependency import for 'date-fns' has been added to the project.

focusAreas.reason: Check that the new humanize function's output format is acceptable for all callers and handles invalid date inputs gracefully.

case.description: Mechanical rename of getCurrentUser to getViewer across five files. No logic changes.

output.summary: The rename from getCurrentUser to getViewer does not alter any functionality. All call sites have been updated consistently. No behavioral changes are introduced.
